zotero.abstractNote: Training data attribution (TDA) aims to quantify the influence of individual training examples on a model's prediction. Existing methods often rely on local linear approximations of the model, which can be inaccurate for deep neural networks. In this work, we propose a Bayesian perspective on TDA. We frame TDA as the problem of estimating the posterior predictive distribution under a leave-one-out training set. We show that this perspective naturally leads to a principled and computationally efficient approach to TDA. We demonstrate that our method, which we call Bayesian TDA, provides more accurate and robust attribution scores than existing methods on a variety of tasks and models.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
